chapter_context.summary: Describes the healing river flowing out from the threshold of the temple eastward, growing deeper until it heals the Dead Sea and gives life to everything it touches, followed by the boundary boundaries of the restored land.

outline.text: The River of Life Flowing Eastward from Temple Threshold
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The River of Life Flowing Eastward from Temple Threshold (47:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Healing Power of Waters and Abundance of Fruitful Trees
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Healing Power of Waters and Abundance of Fruitful Trees (47:6-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: The Boundaries of Divided Land for Twelve Tribes and Strangers
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Boundaries of Divided Land for Twelve Tribes and Strangers (47:13-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies of Hope and Restoration
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies of hope and restoration, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 47, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
